name: Fried Chicken with Ginger Dressing
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Minced green onion, white part only
2 tbsp Minced pared fresh ginger root
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper; rub to coat evenly. Place chicken in a non-aluminum baking pan.
2. Pound 2 onions and the sliced ginger root lightly with the flat side of a cleaver; chop coarsely and scatter over chicken.
3. Sprinkle chicken with rice wine. Marinate at room temperature, turning chicken once, for 30 minutes.
4. Arrange lettuce on a large serving platter and refrigerate covered.
5. Mix sugar, vinegar, stock, soy sauce, and sesame oil in a small saucepan. Stir in minced green onion, ginger, and parsley.
6. Whisk egg white in a bowl until foamy and double in volume. Add 2 tablespoons cornstarch. Reserve the batter.
7. Heat wok over high heat for 20 seconds; add vegetable oil and heat to 350°F.
8. Drain chicken; discard marinade. Pat chicken dry with paper towels; dust with 2 tablespoons cornstarch. Spread batter on areas of chicken not covered with skin, coating evenly.
9. Fry 2 chicken pieces at a time, turning once, until crispy and cooked through, 7 to 9 minutes. (To test for doneness, pierce thickest part of drumstick with the tip of a knife; juices should run clear.) Drain chicken on paper towels, keep warm in a 200°F oven.
10. Repeat frying remaining 2 chicken pieces.
11. Heat stock mixture over medium heat to simmering. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 1 minute. Keep dressing hot.
12. Cut warm chicken into 1/2-inch wide slices using a meat mallet or hammer. Pound through bones with the cleaver or sturdy chef's knife.
13. Reassemble chicken slices and place on bed of lettuce. Pour hot dressing over chicken. Serve immediately.
Makes 4 servings.
